notes.anchor: If Pollex has not yet set out
notes.note: Pollex was one of Cicero's household slaves who served as a letter-carrier and agent during the civil war. Cicero, stranded at Dyrrhachium after Pharsalus, urges Terentia to send him on his way without delay.
The Ides fell on the 13th in July; the letter dates to 15 July 48 BC, written from Dyrrhachium while Cicero awaited the outcome of the Pompeian cause.
